trigger_keywords: do I need a feature flag
what input parameter should be added
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: This is an input validation error. The PRD must explicitly define the name of the boolean feature flag (e.g., 'enable_custom_time_format') and the specific string casing for status codes (e.g., 'running' vs 'RUNNING') before any claim can be made about valid operations.
The constraint implies an order-dependent comparison. The first state code must be 'RUNNING' (return true) and the second 'STOPPED' (return false); any other sequence returns false.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the same mapping pattern as the Host Field request
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Reference a functional requirement for the host.field operation which exists in a PRD section not currently summarized in the constraint table.

context_pointers.vague_reference: reformat timestamps to the same convention used in the Network Field view
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Refer to a specific formatting rule defined in the Network Field section of the PRD, implying identical timestamp handling logic.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_host_info.json.description: Project the daemon root environment document into a single summary object holding the API compatibility level, host trust flag, backing filesystem, driver, daemon version (as a number), runtime version and kernel version.

feature1_2_host_field.json.description: Read a single named environment field from the daemon root; the daemon version field is returned as a floating-point number and the trust field as a boolean.

feature1_3_host_unavailable.json.description: When the daemon read fails, a single-field accessor swallows the error and yields a null result rather than raising.

feature2_1_network_list.json.description: List managed networks, returning only the short name of each from its resource URL.

feature2_2_network_show.json.description: Project a single network document into a summary with its name, type and member short-names.

feature2_3_network_field.json.description: Read one named field (name, type or members) of a single network.

feature3_1_operation_list.json.description: List the daemon's current operation URLs.

feature3_2_operation_info.json.description: Fetch a single operation and return the raw state/document pair.

feature3_3_operation_fields.json.description: Read one projected field of an operation: a reformatted create/update timestamp, or the status string.

feature3_4_operation_actions.json.description: Delete an operation, or wait on it until a target status code or timeout; both return a boolean success flag, and wait encodes its parameters into the request URL.

feature4_10_snapshot_ops.json.description: Create, read, rename or delete a container snapshot; config payloads are sent as JSON bodies.

feature4_11_container_file_get.json.description: Read a file out of a container; the path is carried in the request URL query string.

cases.expected_output:
request=POST /1.0/containers/trusty-1/files?path=dst_file
body=
header.X-LXD-gid=0
header.X-LXD-mode=420
header.X-LXD-uid=0
result=["200", {"metadata": {}, "status": "Success", "status_code": 200, "type": "sync"}]

feature4_12_container_file_put.json.description: Write a file into a container; the destination path is in the URL, the file bytes are the body, and uid/gid/mode are sent as headers.

feature4_13_container_exec.json.description: Execute a command in a container; the command, interactivity flags and environment are sent as a JSON body.

feature4_2_container_running.json.description: Decide whether a container is in an active state from its reported status string.

feature4_3_container_lifecycle.json.description: Drive a container lifecycle transition; the request body carries the action verb, a force flag and a timeout.

feature4_4_container_crud.json.description: Create, replace, destroy or read a container; the supplied spec/config is sent as a JSON body.

feature4_5_container_views.json.description: Read a container's state metadata, its log text, or its full config metadata; the request URL carries the appropriate log query flag.

feature4_6_container_migrate.json.description: Initiate a container migration and return the migration metadata plus the short operation id.

feature4_7_container_defined.json.description: Check whether a container exists, returning a boolean.

feature4_8_container_publish.json.description: Publish a container as a new image; the container reference is sent as a JSON body.

feature5_1_image_list.json.description: List images, or search them with URL-encoded query parameters; both return short fingerprints.

feature5_2_image_defined.json.description: Check whether an image exists: true if found, false on a 404, and a normalized error for any other failure.

feature5_3_image_info.json.description: Project an image document into a summary of dates, public flag, size in MB, fingerprint and architecture name.

feature5_4_image_dates.json.description: Read a single image date (upload/create/expire), reformatting non-zero epoch values and reporting zero as Unknown.

feature5_5_image_fields.json.description: Project a scalar image field (public/size/fingerprint/architecture), fetching metadata when not supplied, and report invalid-size or missing-field errors for bad input.

feature5_6_image_ops.json.description: Delete, replace or rename an image; payloads are sent as JSON bodies and the result is a boolean flag.

feature5_7_image_export.json.description: Export an image, returning its raw bytes.

feature5_8_image_upload.json.description: Upload image bytes to the catalog.

feature6_1_alias_list.json.description: List aliases by short name, or fetch a single alias as a raw response pair.

feature6_2_alias_defined.json.description: Check whether an alias exists, returning a boolean.

feature6_3_alias_ops.json.description: Create, update, rename or delete an alias; payloads are JSON bodies and the result is a boolean flag.

feature7_1_certificate_list.json.description: List certificates by short fingerprint, or fetch a single certificate as a raw response pair.

feature7_2_certificate_ops.json.description: Add or remove a trusted certificate; the create payload is a JSON body and the result is a boolean flag.

feature8_1_profile_list.json.description: List profiles by short name, or fetch a single profile as a raw response pair.

feature8_2_profile_defined.json.description: Check whether a profile exists, returning a boolean.

feature8_3_profile_ops.json.description: Create, update, rename or delete a profile; payloads are JSON bodies and the result is a boolean flag.

feature9_1_response_object.json.description: Translate a raw document response: success pairs for 200 and in-progress 202, a transport error for empty bodies, and an API error for other statuses.

feature9_2_response_status.json.description: Translate a raw status-probe response into a boolean, with empty bodies and error-bearing bodies surfaced as errors.

feature9_3_response_raw.json.description: Translate a raw byte response: the body on 200, otherwise a transport error.

_constraints.output_rule: Construct GET request to '/1.0'. Project response metadata into a summary object.
Construct GET request to '/1.0'. Return the specific field value from metadata/environment.
Construct request anyway. Result field MUST be `null`. Do not throw. Do not output error lines other than the request line.
Construct GET request to '/1.0/networks'. Parse metadata array of URLs. Return array of trailing segments ONLY.
Construct GET request to '/1.0/networks/{network}'. Project metadata into summary object.

_constraints.input_condition: op equals 'networks.field' with params.field and params.network
_constraints.output_rule: Construct GET request to '/1.0/networks/{network}'. Return single field value from metadata.
Construct GET request to '/1.0/operations'. Return metadata array UNCHANGED (verbatim URLs).
Construct GET request (raw read) to '/1.0/operations/{operation}'. Return raw response pair: [status_code, full_doc].
Construct GET to '/1.0/operations/{operation}'. Project specific field (reformat timestamps to 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS', return status string).
Construct DELETE request to '/1.0/operations/{operation}'. Return boolean true.

_constraints.input_condition: op equals 'operations.wait' with params.operation, params.status_code, params.timeout
_constraints.output_rule: Construct GET request to '/1.0/operations/{operation}/wait?status_code={code}&timeout={sec}'. Return boolean true.
Construct GET request to '/1.0/containers'. Return metadata array of URLs projected to trailing segments.
Construct GET request to '/1.0/containers/{container}/state'. Return boolean (true if 'RUNNING', false if 'STOPPED').
Construct PUT request to '/1.0/containers/{container}/state'. Body: {action, force: true, timeout}.

hide.replacement: a specific feature flag or input parameter that must be explicitly designed
